# Identifying Low Competition

transcript.text: So let's talk about identifying low competition spaces and what low competition spaces look
like in local SEO versus high competition, because remember for local SEO, there's two
things that are going to determine the competition, right?
Number one, the service and number two, the population of the area.
So some services like personal injury lawyer are going to be much more competitive than
other ones like plumber and plumber is one of the more competitive ones also.
But a personal injury lawyer, even in a relatively small town, is going to be very, very competitive.
Now plumber Chicago, of course, this is going to be highly competitive, but I just want
to give you an example of what to look for for high competitive space.
All right.
So the first thing we see is we have a lot of reviews, right 900, 200, 6000.
Now reviews aren't a massive rank signal, but they are massive signal for click through.
So even if you manage to get ranked in here with no reviews, you're still not going to
get any calls.
Right?
Like if it's 900 reviews, 200 reviews and you with four, no one's going to call you.
They're going to call these other two.
So legitimately speaking, you need to be able to plan to get at least as many reviews as
what you're seeing in the map pack.
And these are big numbers.
But beyond that, from a peer SEO perspective, let's see, we have first Chicago and they're
right here.

pages, their businesses ranking here, that's typically a sign that it is very competitive.

all three of them, not on the first page of organic results.
That's usually a really, really good sign that it's fairly low competition.

And it's funny that this is heating, cooling, plumbing.

they all have websites, but we don't see them ranking organically right here.

I guarantee this is pronounced so sure.

Very low competition.

And if we look for pounds, okay, they're the number two organic, but they also are the

Long Beach, CJ's doesn't have a location or website, no website, and again, 1277, Gordon

And if we look for Gordon Plumbing, they're not on the first page.
